zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret de Beaumont於1154年8月12日出生在英國英格蘭萊希斯特斯爾萊希斯特 馮 卡素 維福時，她的父親Robert de Beaumont 3rd Earl Of Leicester33歲，母親Petronella de Grandmesnil20歲。她於1179年與Saer de Quincy 1st Earl of Winchester在英國哈普爾維古姆結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1235年1月12日在英國諾薩普頓史爾布拉可利享年80歲，埋葬在英國萊希斯特斯爾加倫登。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Beaumont
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Gilles, Gaetan, Honore, Jacques, Jean-Guy, Marcel.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of the five places in Normandy or several others elsewhere in France so named. The placename comes from Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + mont ‘hill’. There are also places in England so named under Norman influence in Cumberland, Lancashire, and Essex, the last of which changed its name in the 12th century from Fulepet ‘foul pit’ to Bealmont ‘beautiful hill’; these may also have given rise to the surname. The surname is now widespread throughout England, but most common in Yorkshire. Compare Belmont .

History: Many English bearers of this surname are descended from Henry de Beaumont (died 1340), who came to England from France as a soldier, serving Kings Edward I, II, and III of England. He acquired the earldom of Buchan in Scotland by marriage. — Many American bearers of this surname are descendants of John Beaumont (1612–47), who came to North America from England in 1630. William Beaumont came from England to Cambridge MA in 1635 and subsequently moved to CT.

variants.name: Bement
meanings: English (Cambridgeshire): variant of   Beaumont  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Beeman
meanings: English (Kent): occupational name for a beekeeper, from Middle English bee-man ‘bee-keeper’ (Old English bēo + mann).
English (Kent): variant of   Beaumont  .
Americanized form of German Biemann or of its Dutch cognate Bieman.

variants.name: Belmont
meanings: French and English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beaumont  .
Catalan: habitational name from the placename Bellmont ‘beautiful mountain’. Compare Spanish   Belmonte  .

variants.name: Baumunk
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Siegmund.
German: probably a Germanized form of a French Huguenot name Beaumont (see also Beaumont ).

variants.name: Bayman
meanings: English (mainly Lancashire): variant of   Beaumont  .
Americanized form of German Beumann or Bäumann, variants of Baumann .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret de Beaumont was born on 12 August 1154, in Leicester the Castle View, Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Robert de Beaumont 3rd Earl Of Leicester, was 33 and her mother, Petronella de Grandmesnil, was 20. She married Saer de Quincy 1st Earl of Winchester in 1179, in Winchester, Hampshire, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 4 daughters. She died on 12 January 1235, in Brackley, Northamptonshire, England, at the age of 80, and was buried in Garendon, Leicestershire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret de Beaumont est née le 12 août 1154, à Leicester the Castle View, Leicestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Robert de Beaumont 3rd Earl Of Leicester, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Petronella de Grandmesnil, avait 20 ans. Elle a épousé Saer de Quincy 1st Earl of Winchester en 1179, à Winchester, Hampshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée le 12 janvier 1235, à Brackley, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 80 ans, et a été enterrée à Garendon, Leicestershire, Angleterre.

August 1154 in Leicester the Castle View, Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Robert de Beaumont 3rd Earl Of Leicester 33 und ihre Mutter Petronella de Grandmesnil 20. Sie hat 1179 in Winchester, Hampshire, England Saer de Quincy 1st Earl of Winchester geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb am 12. Januar 1235 im Alter von 80 Jahren in Brackley, Northamptonshire, England und wurde in Garendon, Leicestershire, England begraben.

ko.Groby, Leicester Castle, Leicestershire,: 영국 잉글랜드 레이스스터실르 레이스스터 테 카스틀 비w
ko.lifeSummary: Margaret de Beaumont(이)가 1154년 8월 12일 영국 잉글랜드 레이스스터실르 레이스스터 테 카스틀 비w에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert de Beaumont 3rd Earl Of Leicester(은)는 33세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Petronella de Grandmesnil(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 1179년 영국 함프실르 윈체스터에서 Saer de Quincy 1st Earl of Winchester(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1235년 1월 12일 영국 노르섬프토실르 브라클리에서 80세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 레이스스터실르 가던에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com Saer de Quincy 1st Earl of Winchester em 1179, em Winchester, Hampshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 4 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret de Beaumont родилась 12 августа 1154 г. в местности лисестир т кастл вию, лисестиршир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Robert de Beaumont 3rd Earl Of Leicester было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Petronella de Grandmesnil, 20. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Saer de Quincy 1st Earl of Winchester в 1179 г. в местности винчестир, хампшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 4 дочери. Она умерла 12 января 1235 г. в местности браккли, нортамптоншир, Англия в возрасте 80 лет и была похоронена в местности гарендон, лисестиршир, Англия.

Se casó con Saer de Quincy 1st Earl of Winchester en 1179, en Winchester, Hampshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 4 hijas.
